payload.answer: Recall favored mem_new because both memories were relevant to Sam in the Goldman context, but mem_new is the newer/current version in that fact scope. Brain suppresses superseded predecessors during recall so outdated facts are not presented as current. As a result, mem_old was matched but filtered out, and mem_new was returned.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current memory for the Sam + Goldman query scope, and it filtered mem_old because mem_old is marked as superseded by a newer memory covering the same fact slot.

entity_resolution.reason: The recall explanation is based on the query context 'Sam' constrained by 'Goldman'; no canonical entity ID is required to explain why the newer record won.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active/current memory selected for Sam from Goldman.
mem_old matched the same Sam + Goldman context but was excluded from recall because it is superseded and therefore not current.

details: Explained selection of mem_new as the active record.
Explained exclusion of mem_old due to supersession.
Did not restate the superseded memory as a current fact.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded_by link to mem_new.
Check whether both memories were normalized to the same Sam + Goldman entity scope.
Inspect currentness/ranking filters to confirm superseded memories are excluded at recall time.
Review timestamps or revision ordering if the supersession edge is missing or disputed.
